tech_stack: Azure Container Apps (Consumption plan, 2-3 apps: API, ingestion worker, frontend)
Azure Functions (Consumption plan) for document parsing/chunking
Azure OpenAI Service S0 (pay-as-you-go, gpt-4o-mini primary + gpt-4o fallback)
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server - Burstable B1ms (1 vCore, 2 GiB)
Azure AI Search Basic S1 (15 GiB partition)
Azure Blob Storage Standard LRS (Hot, documents corpus)
Azure Key Vault Standard
Azure Active Directory (existing tenant, free tier)
Application Insights + Log Analytics (Pay-as-you-go, 5 GiB cap)
Azure API Management - Consumption tier
GitHub Actions (existing, 2k free min/mo for private)
Region: West Europe only (GDPR data residency)
COA.description: A serverless-first Azure architecture deployed solely in West Europe to keep all EU personal data in-region for GDPR. Java Spring Boot API and React frontend run on Azure Container Apps (Consumption) so we pay only per request with no idle VMs. Document ingestion is event-driven through Azure Functions -> Blob Storage -> Azure AI Search, with retrieval-augmented generation via Azure OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini by default, gpt-4o on escalation). A Burstable PostgreSQL B1ms holds lightweight session/audit metadata, while AAD handles auth and Key Vault stores secrets.
COA.reasoning: Chose Azure Container Apps Consumption over App Service Premium or AKS to eliminate idle compute cost for a 25-user internal tool with bursty traffic. Selected pay-as-you-go Azure OpenAI over a PTU commitment: at 25 internal users, PTU (~ $2k+/mo) would be wildly over-provisioned; we estimate <$400/mo on gpt-4o-mini. Burstable B1ms (~ $30/mo) handles 25-user metadata workload without paying for provisioned IOPS we won't use. AI Search Basic replaces a self-hosted vector DB (no ops, no HA overhead). Single-region deployment halves egress and removes cross-region replication fees. We accept modest single-AZ risk in exchange for ~40-60% lower steady-state cost vs. zone-redundant equivalents.

tech_stack: Azure Front Door Premium + Azure CDN (edge routing, WAF, TLS termination, EU POPs)
AKS (Azure Kubernetes Service) — Standard tier, 3 dedicated node pools: (a) Spring Boot API — D8s_v5 x6 (zone-balanced), (b) AI inference/embedding workers — NC A10 v5 x2, (c) system pods — D4s_v5 x3
Azure OpenAI Service — Provisioned Throughput Units (PTU) on GPT-4o, 2 PTUs baseline + autoscale, deployed in Sweden Central (EU residency)
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server — Premium SSD v2, zone-redundant HA, 8 vCore, 2 read replicas for analytics/search
pgvector on primary + replicas for vector retrieval (co-located for low latency)
Azure Cache for Redis — Premium P2, zone-redundant, cluster mode
Blob Storage — Premium hot tier with lifecycle mgmt; documents chunked and embedded asynchronously
Azure Event Grid + Service Bus Premium (for indexing/decoupling)
Azure Key Vault Premium (HSM-backed keys), Private Endpoints for all PaaS
Entra ID (Azure AD) + Conditional Access + Managed Identity, Defender for Cloud P2, Microsoft Sentinel
Azure Monitor + Log Analytics + App Insights + Container Insights, Diagnostic Settings to immutable storage account (WORM) for audit
POA.description: Edge requests terminate at Front Door Premium with WAF, then route to a zone-redundant AKS cluster in Sweden Central running Spring Boot microservices. The API path is latency-critical: it hits Redis Premium for session/conversation cache, queries the HA PostgreSQL (with pgvector) through a Private Endpoint for retrieval-augmented context, and calls Azure OpenAI over a private link with reserved PTU capacity to eliminate cold starts. A separate GPU node pool handles high-throughput embeddings and batch reindexing off the hot path. The control plane scales via AKS Cluster Autoscaler + HPA, while AI capacity scales via PTU autoscale and additional CPU/GPU pods. EU data residency, encryption at rest with CMK, and full audit/log pipelines satisfy GDPR and security mandates.
POA.reasoning: Dedicated AKS nodes over Functions/Container Apps because persistent connections, JIT-free steady-state CPU, and predictable p95 latency matter for chat UX. Premium SSD v2 + zone-redundant HA + read replicas keep retrieval under tens of ms and absorb peak. Redis Premium removes app-tier load from the database (huge throughput win for repeated/similar queries). Reserved Azure OpenAI PTUs remove token-bucket throttling — the dominant latency variable in shared deployments — and ensure SLA-grade throughput for 25-person team and future growth. GPU node pool is dedicated (not spot) to keep embedding/index latency stable. Front Door Premium provides anycast edge + WAF, minimizing TLS and routing hops. Horizontal scaling is multi-axis: AKS HPA on CPU/RPS, KEDA on Service Bus queue depth for indexing, Postgres read replicas for fan-out, Redis cluster shards, and OpenAI PTU autoscale. Premium networking (private links, zone redundancy) reduces tail-latency variance.

tech_stack: Azure WEU/Sweden North region (EU Data Boundary)
AKS (private cluster, no public API)
Azure App Service (ILF, VNet integrated)
Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible (Private Endpoint, CMK)
Azure AI Search (Private Endpoint, CMK)
Azure OpenAI (Private Endpoint, content filter, abuse monitoring)
Premium Blob Storage (Private Endpoint, CMK, immutable WORM)
Azure Key Vault Premium (HSM, RBAC, Private Endpoint)
Centralized Log Analytics + immutable Archive, Defender for Cloud P2, Microsoft Sentinel
Azure AD Conditional Access + PIM, Azure Policy (Deny, DeployIfNotExist), Microsoft Purview DLP, Front Door WAF
SACA.description: Private VNet with segregated subnets hosts AKS, App Service, Private Endpoints to all data plane services. Azure AD SSO with Conditional Access; Key Vault Premium HSM with CMK for all data-at-rest. Azure OpenAI inference with Prompt Shield, content filters, abuse monitoring. Logs routed to Log Analytics, mirrored immutable to storage, ingested into Sentinel.
SACA.reasoning: EU regions enforce data residency. Private Endpoints eliminate public data-plane attack surface. CMK + HSM-keyed DEK (double encryption) protects data at rest. Conditional Access + PIM enforce least privilege. Azure Policy + Defender enforce baselines. Sentinel + immutable logs enable tamper-evident audit.
